entities.name: Munich Handbook (CLM 849)

attributes.description: Manuscript containing the experiments described in this chunk

entities.raw_quote: This experiment begins a section that is labelled on fol. 96v as follows: Incipiunt experimenta verissima et probata...

entities.raw_quote: ...cultelli et nomen pueri... manubrium sit factum de cornu nigro uel albo.

entities.name: boy (puer virgineus)

entities.name: fingernail divination
entities.raw_quote: Experimentum verissimum et probatum... ad omnia indaganda que volueris, siue presencia, siue preterita, siue futura...

entities.raw_quote: ...ostendensi vngwem istius pueri virginei oleo linito crescere... videre in eo furem et furtum factum nobis ablatum...

summary: This chunk details two necromantic experiments from the Munich Handbook (CLM 849) involving scrying into a boy’s anointed fingernail to reveal thieves and stolen goods. It includes extensive demon conjurations invoking Christological names, Passion motifs, angelic hierarchies, and ecclesiastical authorities. The rituals require protective circles, ritual tools (sword, knife, tripod stool), olive oil, and strict procedural steps. Named spirits include Astaroth, Belzebub, Berit, and multiple demonic entities with Greek-sounding names, alongside divine names like Tetragramaton and Alpha et O.
key_passages: Vmon, Progemon, Mithiomo, Pist, Vralchim, Althes, Panite, Fabar, Thobar, Cormes, Felsmes, Diles, Dilia, Dies, Onaris, coniuro vos demones prenominatos...
Coniuro vos, principes omnium demoniorum, Astaroth, Belzebub, Berit, Fornicator, Temptator, et Seductor, Possessor hominum, Natheus, Molbet.
Per ista lxxija nomina Christi precipio vobis vt michi sitis obedientes et isto puero virgineo...
Accipe puerum virginem de legittimo thoro, et fac 3es circulos.
Hec sunt nomina que scribi debent: in nomine Progeneri, Mutimo, Possunt, Thomopest, Vtany, Votanes...
